raw_question: Among adults with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, how would a computable cohort for SGLT2 inhibitor research be defined, and what external evidence is available?

pico.population: Adults (≥18 years) with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and chronic kidney disease (CKD stages 1–5)
pico.population_detail: Patients with ≥1 ICD-10-CM code for T2DM (E11.*) AND ≥1 ICD-10-CM code for CKD (N18.1–N18.5 or N18.9) within a 12-month lookback period; age ≥18 at index date; enrolled in the source data system ≥12 months before index
pico.intervention: SGLT2 inhibitors: empagliflozin (RxNorm 1545653), dapagliflozin (RxNorm 1488564), canagliflozin (RxNorm 1373458), or ertugliflozin (RxNorm 2043678); identified by ≥1 qualifying prescription at or after index date
pico.timeframe: 12-month minimum lookback period; observation window configurable (MVP default: 24 months post-index)
pico.study_intent: Retrospective descriptive cohort characterization using synthetic FHIR R4 patient data; not a comparative effectiveness study

ambiguity_flags.description: No active comparator arm is specified. The question describes cohort definition only, not a comparative effectiveness analysis.
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: If comparative effectiveness is desired, specify a comparator class (e.g., GLP-1 receptor agonists, DPP-4 inhibitors, sulfonylureas) and define the new-user active-comparator design.

ambiguity_flags.description: Outcomes are listed as research targets but not operationalized with specific coding logic or thresholds.
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: Define eGFR thresholds for CKD progression (e.g., ≥40% decline from baseline or reaching <15 mL/min/1.73m²). Specify HF hospitalization codes (ICD-10-CM I50.*) and required primary discharge position.

ambiguity_flags.description: Observation window and follow-up period are not specified, making attrition and event rates uninterpretable.
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: Define: (1) index date (first qualifying SGLT2 Rx), (2) required continuous enrollment before index, (3) follow-up end date or censoring rules.

ambiguity_flags.description: CKD staging criterion is ambiguous — it is unclear whether eGFR-based staging or diagnosis-code-based staging should be used, or both.
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: Specify whether CKD stage should be confirmed by eGFR lab values (preferred) or ICD-10 codes alone. Define the required number of diagnoses and temporal spacing.

clarifying_questions: Should patients be required to have both T2DM and CKD diagnoses within the same encounter, or within a defined lookback period with no proximity requirement?
Should SGLT2 inhibitor exposure be defined by first-ever prescription (incident use) or any prescription (prevalent use)?
Should patients with end-stage renal disease (N18.6) or on dialysis (CPT 90935, 90937) be excluded from the cohort?
Is a washout period required before the index date to ensure new-user design?
Should baseline eGFR laboratory values be required (potentially excluding patients with missing labs)?
Should patients with type 1 diabetes (E10.*) be explicitly excluded, or rely on T2DM codes only?
